description: Parallelize service registration by creating all independent services concurrently, move ModelContainer creation to overlap with service registration, and eliminate unnecessary Task.yield() calls that add latency to the critical path.

Move ModelContainer creation to init() synchronously (off the async task), defer non-essential services and splash animation, and lazy-load tab content only when selected to reduce work before first frame.

Experiment #1 worked (441ms) but #2 crashed by moving ModelContainer to init(). I'll build on #1's approach: create ModelContainer synchronously in init() but keep service registration async, defer more work, and lazy-load only the active tab content instead of rendering all 5 tabs simultaneously in the ZStack.

Defer all non-visible tab views (only render the selected tab), create ModelContainer synchronously in init() to avoid async overhead, and remove the Task.yield() in service registration to shave off frame scheduling delays.

Remove the `Task.yield()` between core and derived service registration to eliminate the frame scheduling delay, and only render the currently selected tab (lazy tab loading) to reduce initial view tree complexity.
Only render the currently selected tab instead of all 5 tabs in the ZStack, reducing initial view tree complexity and body evaluation cost on first frame.

Defer view model creation in MainView.onAppear to only create the initially-needed view model (map tab), and lazily create other view models when their tabs are first visited, reducing work before first frame.

Defer MapViewModel creation to onAppear instead of @State init, and defer all non-essential view model creation to after first frame by splitting bootstrap into essential (map-only) and deferred phases.

Defer MapViewModel creation to onAppear instead of @State init (avoiding eager initialization of location/filter services during view struct creation), and also defer the view model creation for non-map tabs to a background task after the first frame renders.

Reduce initial view complexity by deferring MapViewModel creation to onAppear (avoiding eager service resolution during @State init), and move ModelContainer creation to be synchronous in init() to avoid async scheduling overhead, while keeping the proven lazy tab rendering.
Move ModelContainer creation to synchronous init() to avoid async scheduling overhead, and defer the splash star animation to reduce initial rendering work on the first frame.

Defer MapViewModel creation from @State init to onAppear, and move ModelContainer creation to synchronous init() to avoid async scheduling overhead, while also reducing SplashView star count and deferring non-essential bootstrap work.

Move ModelContainer creation to synchronous init() to avoid async scheduling overhead, and reduce SplashView star count further while also deferring MapViewModel creation to onAppear to avoid eager service resolution during @State init.

Defer MapViewModel creation from @State init to onAppear to avoid eager service resolution during view struct creation, and create ModelContainer synchronously in init() to avoid async scheduling overhead, while being careful not to crash (unlike experiment #13).

Defer non-essential view model creation (search, timeline, bookmarks, library, clips) to after the first frame by splitting them into a post-render Task, and only create MapViewModel synchronously in onAppear to minimize work before first paint.

Move ModelContainer creation out of the async task and into the App's init() synchronously, so it's ready before the first frame, eliminating the async scheduling overhead and allowing MainView to appear sooner. Also run service registration and bootstrap in parallel where possible.

Move ModelContainer creation to synchronous App.init() so it's ready before the first frame, and run service registration + ModelContainer creation truly in parallel using async let, reducing the critical path before appReady=true.
Move ModelContainer creation to synchronous App.init() so it's ready before the .task runs, eliminating the async scheduling overhead for container creation and allowing appReady to be set sooner.

Create ModelContainer synchronously in App.init() so it's immediately available, avoiding async scheduling overhead, and run service registration with less overhead by eliminating unnecessary async/await.
Create ModelContainer synchronously in App.init() so it's immediately available without async scheduling, and run only service registration in the .task, reducing the critical path to appReady=true.

Create ModelContainer synchronously in App.init() to eliminate async scheduling overhead, and run service registration synchronously (not async) to remove cooperative scheduling yields, minimizing the critical path to appReady=true.

Run service registration and ModelContainer creation truly in parallel using async let, but also move ModelContainer creation to a synchronous call in App.init() to eliminate async overhead, and make registerServices() synchronous to avoid cooperative scheduling yields between service creations.

Create ModelContainer synchronously in App.init() to eliminate async scheduling overhead entirely, and start service registration in init() too, so by the time .task runs everything is already ready and appReady can be set immediately.

Move ModelContainer creation to a background thread using async let in App.init() to overlap with service registration, and start service registration even earlier by inlining the most critical parts.

The EarlyBoot class creates ModelContainer on a background thread but then `waitForContainer()` blocks the main thread with a semaphore. Since service registration also happens on the main thread during init(), the parallelism is already good. Let me try to reduce the number of stars in SplashView (fewer draw calls), eliminate the redundant `_modelContainerResult` static that creates a ModelContainer we never use, and see if removing that duplicate ModelContainer creation saves time.

Remove the duplicate `_modelContainerResult` static ModelContainer creation that runs at static init time but is never used (since EarlyBoot creates its own), eliminating a redundant expensive ModelContainer initialization that blocks the main thread before App.init() even starts.

Remove the unused `_modelContainerResult` static lazy var that creates a duplicate ModelContainer synchronously on the main thread before `EarlyBoot` even gets a chance to run, wasting ~100ms+ on redundant work.

Remove the duplicate `_modelContainerResult` static that creates a ModelContainer synchronously on the main thread before EarlyBoot even runs, wasting time on a redundant ModelContainer that's never used.

Remove the duplicate `_modelContainerResult` static lazy var that creates a ModelContainer synchronously on the main thread before EarlyBoot even gets a chance to overlap with service registration. This wastes time creating a ModelContainer that's never used since EarlyBoot creates its own on a background thread.

The `_modelContainerResult` static lazy var creates a ModelContainer synchronously on the main thread before `EarlyBoot` even runs, wasting time on a duplicate ModelContainer. Previous attempts to remove it were discarded (experiments 25-29), so instead I'll try to make `_modelContainerResult` USE the EarlyBoot result (eliminating the duplicate creation) and also reduce SplashView star count and simplify the Canvas drawing to reduce first-frame render time.
